---
title: "Model features"
output:
  rmarkdown::html_vignette:
    toc: true
    number_sections: true
bibliography: library.bib
csl: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/citation-style-language/styles/master/apa-numeric-superscript-brackets.csl
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Model features}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

```{r, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  collapse = TRUE,
  comment = "#>"
)
```

This vignette provides a quick reference to the modelling features
available in EpiNow2.
For mathematical details see the model definition vignettes
([infection model](estimate_infections.html),
[secondary model](estimate_secondary.html),
[truncation model](estimate_truncation.html)).
For applied examples see the
[options vignette](estimate_infections_options.html).
For prior guidance see the
[prior choice guide](prior_choice_guide.html).

# Component overview

| Component | R function | Purpose | Details |
|---|---|---|---|
| Infection model | `estimate_infections()` | Estimate infections, Rt, and growth rate from reported cases | [Infection model](#infection-model) |
| Secondary model | `estimate_secondary()` | Estimate secondary outcomes from primary observations | [Secondary model](#secondary-model) |
| Truncation model | `estimate_truncation()` | Estimate reporting truncation from data snapshots | [Truncation](#truncation) |
| Forecasting | `forecast_infections()`, `forecast_secondary()` | Generate forecasts from fitted models | [Forecasting](#forecasting) |
| Workflow wrappers | `epinow()`, `regional_epinow()` | Production and multi-region workflows | [Wrappers](#workflow-wrappers) |

# Infection model

`estimate_infections()` reconstructs infections from reported cases
using either a generative renewal model or non-parametric
back-calculation.

| Feature | Argument | Description | See also |
|---|---|---|---|
| Renewal equation (default) | `rt = rt_opts(...)` | Generative model using the reproduction number and generation time | [Model definition](estimate_infections.html) |
| Back-calculation | `rt = NULL`, `backcalc = backcalc_opts(...)` | Non-parametric deconvolution of reported cases | [Model definition](estimate_infections.html) |

See also: [estimate_infections workflow](estimate_infections_workflow.html)

# Reproduction number

When using the renewal equation, several options control how the
time-varying reproduction number is modelled.

| Feature | Argument | Description | See also |
|---|---|---|---|
| GP on differences of log Rt (default) | `rt_opts(gp_on = "R_t-1")` | Gaussian process applied to successive Rt values | [Options examples](estimate_infections_options.html) |
| GP on deviations from R0 | `rt_opts(gp_on = "R0")` | Gaussian process applied as deviations from a global mean | [Options examples](estimate_infections_options.html) |
| Random walk | `rt_opts(rw = 7)` with `gp = NULL` | Piecewise-constant Rt with specified step size | [Options examples](estimate_infections_options.html) |
| Breakpoints | `rt_opts(use_breakpoints = TRUE)` | Requires a `breakpoint` column in the input data | [Options examples](estimate_infections_options.html) |
| Fixed Rt | `gp = NULL`, `rt_opts(rw = 0)` | Constant reproduction number (no GP or random walk) | [Options examples](estimate_infections_options.html) |
| Population adjustment | `rt_opts(pop = Fixed(N))` | Adjust Rt for susceptible depletion | [Options examples](estimate_infections_options.html) |

See also:
[estimate_infections model definition](estimate_infections.html),
[options examples](estimate_infections_options.html)

# Gaussian process

The Gaussian process controls the flexibility of the time-varying
reproduction number or infection trajectory.

| Feature | Argument | Description | See also |
|---|---|---|---|
| Matern 3/2 (default) | `gp_opts(kernel = "matern")` | Default kernel with `matern_order = 3/2` | [GP implementation details](gaussian_process_implementation_details.html) |
| Squared exponential | `gp_opts(kernel = "se")` | Infinitely differentiable kernel | [GP implementation details](gaussian_process_implementation_details.html) |
| Ornstein-Uhlenbeck | `gp_opts(kernel = "ou")` | Matern kernel with order 1/2 | [GP implementation details](gaussian_process_implementation_details.html) |
| Periodic | `gp_opts(kernel = "periodic")` | Periodic kernel with tuneable frequency `w0` | [GP implementation details](gaussian_process_implementation_details.html) |
| Disable GP | `gp = NULL` in `estimate_infections()` | Remove the Gaussian process entirely | [Options examples](estimate_infections_options.html) |
| Accuracy/speed tradeoff | `gp_opts(basis_prop = 0.3)` | Higher values increase accuracy; lower values are faster | [GP implementation details](gaussian_process_implementation_details.html) |

See also:
[GP implementation details](gaussian_process_implementation_details.html),
[options examples](estimate_infections_options.html)

# Delay distributions

Delay distributions map latent infections to observed quantities.

| Feature | Argument | Description | See also |
|---|---|---|---|
| Generation time | `gt_opts(dist = Gamma(...))` | Time between successive infections | [Prior choice guide](prior_choice_guide.html) |
| Reporting delay | `delay_opts(dist = LogNormal(...))` | Delay from infection to report | [Prior choice guide](prior_choice_guide.html) |
| Composite delays | `delay_opts(dist = dist1 + dist2)` | Sum of independent delay distributions | [Workflow](estimate_infections_workflow.html) |
| Non-parametric delays | `gt_opts(dist = NonParametric(...))` | Fixed PMF rather than a parametric family | [Workflow](estimate_infections_workflow.html) |
| Uncertain parameters | `LogNormal(meanlog = Normal(...), sdlog = Normal(...))` | Parameters drawn from prior distributions | [Prior choice guide](prior_choice_guide.html) |
| Truncation correction | `trunc_opts(dist = LogNormal(...))` | Adjust for right-truncation of recent data | [Truncation](#truncation) |

See also:
[workflow vignette](estimate_infections_workflow.html),
[prior choice guide](prior_choice_guide.html)

# Observation model

The observation model links latent expected cases to reported data.

| Feature | Argument | Description | See also |
|---|---|---|---|
| Negative binomial (default) | `obs_opts(family = "negbin")` | Overdispersed count model | [Prior choice guide](prior_choice_guide.html) |
| Poisson | `obs_opts(family = "poisson")` | No overdispersion | [Prior choice guide](prior_choice_guide.html) |
| Day-of-week effect | `obs_opts(week_effect = TRUE)` | Separate reporting rate for each day of the week | [Prior choice guide](prior_choice_guide.html) |
| Scaling / ascertainment | `obs_opts(scale = Normal(0.5, 0.1))` | Fraction of infections observed | [Prior choice guide](prior_choice_guide.html) |
| Accumulation (weekly data) | `fill_missing()` with `accumulate` column | Aggregate daily expected values to match reporting frequency | [Workflow](estimate_infections_workflow.html) |
| Likelihood weighting | `obs_opts(weight = ...)` | Re-weight the observed data in the log density | [Prior choice guide](prior_choice_guide.html) |

See also:
[workflow vignette](estimate_infections_workflow.html),
[prior choice guide](prior_choice_guide.html)

# Secondary model

`estimate_secondary()` estimates the relationship between a primary
and secondary observation (e.g. cases and deaths, admissions and
bed occupancy).

| Feature | Argument | Description | See also |
|---|---|---|---|
| Incidence model | `secondary_opts(type = "incidence")` | Secondary reports as a convolution of primary cases | [Secondary model definition](estimate_secondary.html) |
| Prevalence model | `secondary_opts(type = "prevalence")` | Cumulative secondary reports (e.g. bed occupancy) | [Secondary model definition](estimate_secondary.html) |

See also:
[estimate_secondary model definition](estimate_secondary.html)

# Truncation

`estimate_truncation()` estimates a truncation distribution from
multiple snapshots of the same data source over time.

| Feature | Argument | Description | See also |
|---|---|---|---|
| Estimate truncation | `estimate_truncation(data = list_of_snapshots)` | Fit a log-normal truncation distribution to snapshot data | [Truncation model definition](estimate_truncation.html) |
| Apply truncation correction | `trunc_opts(dist = ...)` in `estimate_infections()` | Adjust for right-truncation using an estimated or assumed distribution | [Truncation model definition](estimate_truncation.html) |

See also:
[estimate_truncation model definition](estimate_truncation.html)

# Forecasting

Forecasts can be generated from fitted models by projecting forward
with specified or estimated parameters.

| Feature | Argument | Description | See also |
|---|---|---|---|
| Infection forecast | `forecast_infections(estimates, R)` | Simulate future infections from a fitted model with a new Rt trajectory | [Workflow](estimate_infections_workflow.html) |
| Secondary forecast | `forecast_secondary(estimate, primary)` | Forecast secondary observations from new primary data | [Secondary model definition](estimate_secondary.html) |

See also:
[workflow vignette](estimate_infections_workflow.html)

# Workflow wrappers

Convenience functions wrap the core estimation and reporting steps.

| Feature | Argument | Description | See also |
|---|---|---|---|
| Production wrapper | `epinow()` | Wraps `estimate_infections()` with logging and formatted output | [epinow vignette](epinow.html) |
| Multi-region | `regional_epinow()` | Runs `epinow()` across regions in parallel | [Getting started](EpiNow2.html) |
| Region-specific options | `opts_list()` | Generate per-region configuration lists | [Getting started](EpiNow2.html) |

See also:
[epinow vignette](epinow.html),
[getting started](EpiNow2.html)

# Stan backend

Models are implemented in Stan.
Users can switch between MCMC sampling (default, via `cmdstanr` or
`rstan`), variational inference, the Laplace approximation, or
pathfinder using `stan_opts(method = ...)`.
See the [workflow vignette](estimate_infections_workflow.html) for
details on configuring the backend.
